{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Station Cashier - Female",
  "description": "Virgin Forest Energy Limited is a wholly-owned and well capitalized Nigerian energy downstream oil trading company. We are dedicated to the provision of top-rate petroleum products and by-products to our vast array of clients and customers across Nigeria and beyond. In the course of our short but eventful existence as a corporate entity, we have carved a niche for ourselves as a company sensitive to the peculiar environment in which we operate, and we have tailored our operations in response to the ever-changing demands of a vibrant and dynamic emerging economy such as Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Station Cashier - Female

Job Location: Enugu&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Station Cashier is responsible for handling all cash and electronic transactions at the station, ensuring accurate billing, proper documentation, and excellent customer service.
	The role requires integrity, attention to detail, and adherence to company financial procedures.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive payments (cash, POS, transfers) for fuel and other products sold
	Issue receipts and ensure accurate recording of all transactions
	Maintain proper cash control and ensure zero discrepancies
	Reconcile daily sales and prepare end-of-shift reports
	Operate POS systems efficiently and accurately
	Attend to customer inquiries and provide prompt service
	Ensure proper documentation of sales (PMS, AGO, LPG, lubricants, etc.)
	Report any cash shortages, overages, or suspicious transactions immediately
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the cashier area
	Support stock tracking by recording product sales accurately
	Work closely with pump attendants and supervisors to ensure smooth operations
	Adhere strictly to company policies, financial controls, and safety standards


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy of transactions and cash balancing
	Zero or minimal cash discrepancies
	Speed and efficiency of service
	Customer satisfaction level
	Compliance with financial procedures


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Minimum of OND / SSCE (additional qualification is an advantage)
	1&ndash;3 years of experience in a cashier or retail role (preferably in oil &amp; gas)
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Familiarity with POS systems and cash handling
	Strong numerical and record-keeping skills
	Good communication and customer service skills
	High level of honesty and integrity


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail.
	Accountability and trustworthiness.
	Customer service orientation
	Time management
	Ability to work under pressure
	Teamwork and cooperation.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@virginforest.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Station Cashier - Female Job at Virgin Forest Energy Limited




        
    

        
        Virgin Forest Energy Limited is a wholly-owned and well capitalized Nigerian energy downstream oil trading company. We are dedicated to the provision of top-rate petroleum products and by-products to our vast array of clients and customers across Nigeria and beyond. In the course of our short but eventful existence as a corporate entity, we have carved a niche for ourselves as a company sensitive to the peculiar environment in which we operate, and we have tailored our operations in response to the ever-changing demands of a vibrant and dynamic emerging economy such as Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Station Cashier - Female

Job Location: Enugu 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Station Cashier is responsible for handling all cash and electronic transactions at the station, ensuring accurate billing, proper documentation, and excellent customer service.
	The role requires integrity, attention to detail, and adherence to company financial procedures.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive payments (cash, POS, transfers) for fuel and other products sold
	Issue receipts and ensure accurate recording of all transactions
	Maintain proper cash control and ensure zero discrepancies
	Reconcile daily sales and prepare end-of-shift reports
	Operate POS systems efficiently and accurately
	Attend to customer inquiries and provide prompt service
	Ensure proper documentation of sales (PMS, AGO, LPG, lubricants, etc.)
	Report any cash shortages, overages, or suspicious transactions immediately
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the cashier area
	Support stock tracking by recording product sales accurately
	Work closely with pump attendants and supervisors to ensure smooth operations
	Adhere strictly to company policies, financial controls, and safety standards


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy of transactions and cash balancing
	Zero or minimal cash discrepancies
	Speed and efficiency of service
	Customer satisfaction level
	Compliance with financial procedures


Qualifications & Requirements


	Minimum of OND / SSCE (additional qualification is an advantage)
	1–3 years of experience in a cashier or retail role (preferably in oil & gas)
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Familiarity with POS systems and cash handling
	Strong numerical and record-keeping skills
	Good communication and customer service skills
	High level of honesty and integrity


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail.
	Accountability and trustworthiness.
	Customer service orientation
	Time management
	Ability to work under pressure
	Teamwork and cooperation.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of HR Operations",
  "description": "Anas Atiantic Services Limited is a dynamic and client-centric business services company registered and operating in Ikeja, Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to delivering high-quality solutions that help businesses grow, compete, and connect in today&rsquo;s fast-changing market.

Founded on the belief that innovation and strategic excellence drive success, Anas Atiantic Services Limited combines creativity, technology, and professionalism to meet the diverse needs of our clients. Our team comprises experienced professionals passionate about delivering results.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of HR Operations
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking for a Head of HR Operations to oversee all personnel-related matters and ensure we use our resources beneficially.


Responsibilities


	Lead HR projects like compensation plans revisions
	Implement functional HRMS and internal databases across all departments and locations
	Oversee our payroll and performance evaluation systems
	Design company policies and procedures
	Review and update our employment contracts and agreements
	Prepare budgets by department
	Track key HR metrics like cost per hire and retention rates
	Manage internal communication projects (like job satisfaction surveys)
	Measure the effectiveness of our benefits programs and recommend improvements
	Coordinate employee training and development initiatives
	Ensure our recordkeeping and data processing procedures comply with GDPR requirements.


Requirements


	Work experience as a Head of HR Operations or similar role
	Knowledge with Human Resources Information Systems including payroll tools
	Knowledge in designing compensation and benefits programs
	Good knowledge of labor legislation
	Leadership abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to:&nbsp;anasatianticserviceslimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Head of HR Operations Job at Anas Atiantic Services Limited




        
    

        
        Anas Atiantic Services Limited is a dynamic and client-centric business services company registered and operating in Ikeja, Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to delivering high-quality solutions that help businesses grow, compete, and connect in today’s fast-changing market.

Founded on the belief that innovation and strategic excellence drive success, Anas Atiantic Services Limited combines creativity, technology, and professionalism to meet the diverse needs of our clients. Our team comprises experienced professionals passionate about delivering results.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of HR Operations
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Description


	We are looking for a Head of HR Operations to oversee all personnel-related matters and ensure we use our resources beneficially.


Responsibilities


	Lead HR projects like compensation plans revisions
	Implement functional HRMS and internal databases across all departments and locations
	Oversee our payroll and performance evaluation systems
	Design company policies and procedures
	Review and update our employment contracts and agreements
	Prepare budgets by department
	Track key HR metrics like cost per hire and retention rates
	Manage internal communication projects (like job satisfaction surveys)
	Measure the effectiveness of our benefits programs and recommend improvements
	Coordinate employee training and development initiatives
	Ensure our recordkeeping and data processing procedures comply with GDPR requirements.


Requirements


	Work experience as a Head of HR Operations or similar role
	Knowledge with Human Resources Information Systems including payroll tools
	Knowledge in designing compensation and benefits programs
	Good knowledge of labor legislation
	Leadership abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
